A Singular Position in Spine Surgery

Where International Mastery
Meets District-Level Dedication.

Very few surgeons in the world hold both the training of a top UK neurosurgical centre and the grounded, high-volume experience of a District Neurosurgeon. Dr. Sultan is one of them.

01
🇬🇧

Trained at Britain's Finest

Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham is one of the United Kingdom's premier neurosurgical and trauma centres. As Former Senior Registrar, Dr. Sultan operated alongside the best in the field — bringing that same standard to every patient he sees today.

02
🏥

District Neurosurgeon — Vast Clinical Experience

As District Neurosurgeon for Jhang, Dr. Sultan has managed an extraordinarily broad and complex caseload serving millions of patients. This high-volume, high-stakes clinical exposure is impossible to replicate in quieter private practices.

03
🌍

Globally Connected, Regionally Committed

Member of the British Association of Spine Surgeons, Society of British Neurological Surgeons, Congress of Neurological Surgery (USA), and European Associations of Neurosurgical Societies — Dr. Sultan stays at the frontier of global spine surgery.

04
📖

Academic & Editorial Leadership

Editorial Board Member of Surgical Neurology International. Core Faculty at the Pakistan Academy of Neurological Surgeons. Executive Committee Member, Pakistan Society of Neurooncology. His expertise shapes the field itself.

05
💬

A Second Opinion That Actually Matters

When you seek a second opinion, the value lies entirely in the independence and calibre of the reviewing surgeon. Dr. Sultan's international training and diverse clinical experience make his opinion genuinely distinctive — not just confirmatory.

06
🔒

Private, Direct & Personalised

Every case is reviewed personally by Dr. Sultan. Not by a registrar. Not by an assistant. You receive his direct clinical opinion, his reasoning, and his recommendation — with the option to discuss it with him personally on a video call.

The Process

Simple, Secure
& Completely Online.

Obtaining an expert second opinion from Dr. Sultan takes less than 10 minutes to initiate. Here is what happens after you submit your request.

1

Submit Your Request

Complete the short intake form with your details, condition, and which service you require.

2

Secure Scan Upload

We send you a secure link to upload your MRI, CT scans, and radiology reports privately.

3

Confirm & Pay

Choose your package and complete payment via card, bank transfer, or international wire.

4

Personal Review

Dr. Sultan personally reviews every image and prepares your detailed clinical opinion report.

5

Receive Your Report

Your expert PDF report is delivered within your agreed timeframe. Video call included in selected packages.

About Dr. Zaeem Sultan

A Surgeon Shaped
by Two Worlds.

"Spine surgery is all about operating on the right patient, at the right time, and only with the right indication."
Dr. Zaeem Sultan is among a rare cohort of Pakistani spine surgeons who have trained to the highest level at a United Kingdom institution and returned to practice. As Former Senior Registrar at Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham — a Major Trauma Centre and one of the UK's foremost neurosurgical units — he gained exposure to complex spinal and neurological cases at a scale and acuity matched by very few centres in the world.

Today, Dr. Sultan serves as District Neurosurgeon for Jhang, managing a vast and clinically diverse patient population across Punjab. This combination — elite international training alongside high-volume district-level surgical practice — produces a depth of clinical judgment that is genuinely uncommon.

He holds membership in leading surgical bodies in the UK, USA, Europe, and Pakistan, and serves on the Editorial Board of Surgical Neurology International and as Core Faculty at the Pakistan Academy of Neurological Surgeons. His practice is grounded in evidence, international standards, and a commitment to giving every patient — wherever they are — access to truly expert guidance.
🎓MBBS — Allama Iqbal Medical College (AIMC)Foundation medical qualification
📜CHPE — Centre for Health Professions Education, LUMSHealthcare professional education
🏅FCPS (Neurosurgery) — College of Physicians & Surgeons PakistanSpecialist neurosurgery fellowship qualification
🇬🇧MRCPS (Glasg) — Royal College of Physicians & Surgeons, GlasgowUK Royal College membership
🏆FRSM — Fellow, Royal Society of Medicine, EnglandDistinguished fellowship of the Royal Society of Medicine
🔬Fellowship in Spine & Neurosurgery — EnglandFormer Senior Registrar, Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham
🧠Diploma in Neurotrauma Care (GN) — AOInternational neurotrauma qualification
🌟Distinguished International Fellow — Neurosurgery, BirminghamAwarded for excellence in neurosurgical training
Clinic Locations

Where to Find
Dr. Sultan.

For in-person consultations and surgical care, Dr. Sultan practises at the following hospitals across Pakistan.

🏥

Farooq Hospital

DHA, Lahore
One of Lahore's premier private hospitals in the prestigious DHA district. Dr. Sultan holds specialist spine and neurosurgery outpatient and surgical sessions here, serving patients across Lahore and Central Punjab.
📍 DHA, Lahore, Punjab
🏨

Buch International Hospital

Multan
A leading tertiary care centre serving Southern Punjab. Dr. Sultan provides specialist spine and neurosurgery consultations and surgical services for patients across Multan and the wider south.
📍 Multan, Punjab
🔬

Sultan Neurospine Service — Toba

Toba Tek Singh, Punjab
Dr. Sultan's own dedicated neurospine service delivering specialist spine and neurosurgery care to patients across the Toba Tek Singh district of Punjab.
📍 Toba Tek Singh, Punjab
🏛️

Sultan Neurospine Service — Jhang

Jhang, Punjab
As District Neurosurgeon for Jhang, Dr. Sultan provides specialist neurosurgical care to one of Punjab's most populous districts — bringing the highest level of training to underserved communities.
📍 Jhang, Punjab
Conditions We Review

Spine Conditions Requiring
Expert Assessment.

Dr. Sultan provides expert second opinions across the full spectrum of spinal and neurosurgical conditions.

💠

Herniated / Slipped Disc

Get an independent expert assessment on whether your disc herniation truly requires surgery, or whether conservative management is the appropriate course.

⚡

Sciatica

Burning or shooting leg pain originating from the spine. Understand the precise underlying cause and the right treatment pathway for your specific case.

🦴

Spinal Stenosis

Narrowing of the spinal canal causing progressive pain and disability. Expert review of whether decompressive surgery is warranted and when.

🔗

Spondylolisthesis

Vertebral slippage causing instability and pain. Understand the grade, significance, and whether surgical fusion is truly the right option for you.

🧠

Spinal Tumours

Complex, high-stakes cases requiring the experience of a surgeon trained in spinal oncology at a major UK centre. Urgent priority available.

↩️

Failed Back Surgery Syndrome

Persistent pain following spinal surgery is one of the most challenging scenarios in spine care. Expert assessment of causation and remaining options.

📐

Scoliosis

Abnormal curvature of the spine that may or may not require intervention. Expert guidance on the right timing, approach, and surgical thresholds.

🚨

Spinal Fractures & Trauma

Trauma cases with vertebral involvement requiring urgent expert assessment. Priority consultation available for time-sensitive decisions.

🔄

Degenerative Disc Disease

Age-related disc changes that are frequently over- or under-treated. Understand exactly what your MRI findings mean and what they do — and don't — require.

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything You
Need to Know.

If your question is not answered here, please WhatsApp us directly and we will respond promptly.

Is a second opinion service legally permissible internationally? +
Providing a professional second opinion — reviewing imaging and records and offering a clinical assessment — is generally permissible internationally without local licensure. This is distinct from prescribing treatment or assuming responsibility for your care. We recommend continuing with your local treating physician for any active treatment, and consulting a local advisor if you have jurisdiction-specific questions.
What documents and scans are required? +
The most useful materials are: MRI or CT scans (ideally DICOM files or high-quality images), the associated radiology report, any prior surgical notes or specialist letters, and a brief history of your symptoms and treatment to date. Don't worry if your records are incomplete — we will advise you on what we can work with after your request is received.
How are my scans transferred securely? +
After you submit your request, we provide you with a secure upload link. Your files are encrypted during transfer and are not shared with any third party. We do not retain your medical files beyond the consultation period. Your privacy is treated as an absolute priority.
What payment methods are accepted? +
We accept international credit and debit cards via Stripe, PayPal, and Wise bank transfer (for receiving USD or GBP). Pakistani patients may also use standard bank transfer or Easypaisa. Payment is completed after your case details are submitted and before Dr. Sultan begins his review.
What exactly will the second opinion report contain? +
Your report will include Dr. Sultan's independent review of your imaging findings, his assessment of the diagnosis, an evaluation of whether the proposed treatment is appropriate, any alternative approaches that should be considered, and clear recommendations for your next steps. Comprehensive and Priority packages include a personal video consultation to discuss the report with Dr. Sultan directly.
Can Dr. Sultan perform my surgery if he recommends it? +
Yes. Dr. Sultan practises in Lahore (Farooq Hospital, DHA), Multan (Buch International Hospital), and across Jhang and Toba districts. If following his review he recommends surgery and you wish to proceed with him, this can be arranged. For international patients unable to travel to Pakistan, he can provide a detailed referral letter for your local surgical team.
In which languages is the service available? +
Consultations, reports, and video calls are available in both English and Urdu. WhatsApp communication is flexible and you are welcome to use whichever language you are most comfortable with.
What if I have further questions after receiving my report? +
Patient satisfaction and clinical clarity are our priorities. If your report does not fully address your questions, please contact us within 48 hours of receipt and we will arrange a complimentary follow-up discussion with Dr. Sultan to address anything that remains unclear.
